copper_deficit_closed.detail: Deficit estimates range 150K-450K tonnes for 2026. LME on-warrant inventory dropped to 89,725mt — lowest in 6 months. Steep backwardation (near > far). Disruptions at mines in Indonesia, Chile, DRC compounding deficit. JP Morgan targets $12,500/mt in Q2 2026.

copper_substitute_found.detail: No viable substitute for copper in electrical wiring, grid infrastructure, or EV motors. Aluminum can substitute in SOME overhead transmission but not in building wiring, data center bus bars, or EV motors. Physics unchanged.

demand_destruction_recession.detail: Copper miners down 8-18% in last month. Tariff uncertainty + recession fears weighing on near-term sentiment. But structural demand (data centers 1.1Mmt/yr by 2030, EV doubling by 2035, grid upgrades) is multi-year. Short-term pullback ≠ demand destruction.

lme_backwardation.detail: LME on-warrant copper at 89,725mt, lowest in 6 months. Steep backwardation means physical copper is scarcer than paper suggests. Same pattern as silver COMEX.

data_center_copper_demand.detail: Sprott: data centers need 1.1Mmt copper/yr by 2030 (~3% global demand). AI training = 58% of DC copper demand by 2030. This is NOT priced in yet.

miner_pullback_opportunity.detail: FCX -7.7%, SCCO -9.1%, TECK -14.3%, HBM -17.7% in last month. Structural thesis intact but short-term sentiment negative. If deficit thesis holds, this is an entry opportunity, not a warning.

dtm_1_2b_acquisition.detail: DTM acquired 3 FERC-regulated pipelines from ONEOK for $1.2B. 3.7 Bcf/d capacity, 1,300 miles across 7 Midwest states. Guardian Pipeline 15% expansion sanctioned for 2028. Midwest power demand growth = data center build zone.

screen_verdict.new_risks: Short-term tariff/recession fear causing miner pullback. Structural demand unchanged.
screen_verdict.action: PAPER TRADE — Miner pullback (-8 to -18% in 1 month) may be entry if deficit thesis holds. MTZ (+8.2% 1M) is the outlier — infrastructure construction doesn't pull back with miners.
